title: regarding the incompatibility of Skopeo and docker together.
body:
I had a query that while using the command : sudo dnf -y install skopeo and we get latest version 1.6.0 . But as requirement we need skopeo 1.6.0  - but if we install 1.6, it is uninstall docker..
We are on CentOS8 which have Docker  Version:  20.10.1 is bring skopeo 1.4.0. so with skopeo 1.6.0 which version of docker will be compatible as we require the skopeo copy --sign-passphrase-file path command which is available on skopeo 1.6.0 not on 1.4.0. Kindly solve the issue as early . thanks in advance.

comments.body:
Skopeo requires containers-common which require the latest runc.  Docker has a hard requirement for a particular version of runc, so these conflict.  Open an issue with Docker to make the version of runc that it ships private to docker and store it somewhere like /usr/libexec/docker/runc.  Docker should not be conflicting with standard distro packages.

Have you looked at Podman.

If you need docker and the latest skopeo, then you could attempt to run skopeo in a privileged container launched by Docker, or just use Podman.  :%)
@rhatdan can you please send the way how to approach to sign and push the image to container registry with the approach of podman tool. so please help us out.

Are you talking about signing it with Docker Notary?  If yes, then this is not supported with Podman or Skopeo for that matter.
Podman and Skopeo support simple signing.
